article_title: Oura Launches Ring 5 as World's Smallest Smart Ring, With Blood Pressure Signals and a $99 Charging Case
summary: Submission approvable with 1 minor warning(s) — file a corrections record on merge

content_preview.title: Oura Launches Ring 5 as World's Smallest Smart Ring, With Blood Pressure Signals and a $99 Charging Case
content_preview.summary: Oura's fifth-generation smart ring is 40% smaller than its predecessor, adds Health Radar software with Blood Pressure Signals, and ships June 4 starting at $399.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Oura on Wednesday unveiled the Ring 5, its fifth-generation smart ring, billing it as the world's smallest smart ring and the company's most significant redesign since the product category emerged as a mainstream alternative to the smartwatch. At 6.09mm wide and 2.28mm thick — down from 7.99mm and 2.88mm on the Ring 4 — the new device is 40 percent smaller than its predecessor, according to [TechCrunch](https://techcrunch.com/2026/05/28/oura-unveils-its-ring-5-with-a-thinner-lighter...

recommendations: Before merging, the human reviewer should write a corrections file at src/content/corrections/<YYYY-MM>/<article-slug>.json documenting the warning(s).
Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-written consumer tech launch article covering the Oura Ring 5. Good structure covering hardware design, software features, pricing, and competitive context. 751 words fits the News category.
editor_notes.source_verification: source-0.html.gz (TechCrunch): confirmed 40% smaller claim, June 4 shipping, preorder info, Ring 5 architecture redesign. source-1.html.gz (9to5Mac): confirmed dimensions (6.09mm x 2.28mm vs Ring 4's 7.99mm x 2.88mm), 'thinner and lighter with a smoother curvature' quote verbatim, Blood Pressure Signals and Nighttime Breathing features. source-2.html.gz (Tech Advisor): confirmed 100m water resistance, 6-9 day battery life, Health Radar availability in US/India/UAE. source-3.html.gz (The Next Web): confirmed 'real technological miracle' CEO Tom Hale quote (attributed to CNBC), Samsung Galaxy Ring 2.3g comparison. source-4.html.gz (MacRumors): confirmed $99 Charging Case, 'traditional wedding band' comparison, Ring 4 Ceramic at $349. source-5.html.gz (IBTimes Australia): confirmed CEO Tom Hale 'By reimagining...' quote, Counsel Health partnership in 43 US states, GLP-1 Insights feature.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: One misattribution found: the article states 'CEO Tom Hale described the product in a statement as a real technological miracle...according to IBTimes Australia.' The 'real technological miracle' quote is actually in The Next Web (source-3), where it's attributed to Tom Hale speaking to CNBC. IBTimes Australia has a different Tom Hale quote about broader access. The article combines both quotes and attributes both to IBTimes, which is incorrect for the first one. The quote itself is real and verified in The Next Web. This is a misattribution of a real quote, recoverable with a correction.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional. Appropriate coverage of a consumer product launch without promotional language.
editor_notes.originality: No duplicate Oura Ring 5 articles found in archive. The internal link to the Pebble Index article exists and is accurate.
concerns: Tom Hale 'real technological miracle' quote misattributed to IBTimes Australia; actually from The Next Web (where it's attributed to CNBC).
recommendations: File corrections note for the misattributed CEO quote.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Solid consumer tech launch article with one misattributed quote (real quote, wrong source). APPROVE_WITH_CORRECTIONS.
